extracted.claim: Dashboards with many charts load very slowly, disrupting the user's workflow.
extracted.verbatim_quote: it can take 30 seconds to load. I literally make coffee while I wait sometimes.

extracted.verbatim_quote: they sent me a CSV breakdown but it didn't tie out to what's in the billing page in the app. So I just kind of... gave up on understanding it.

extracted.claim: The pricing structure is opaque and users cannot understand why they are billed a certain amount.
extracted.verbatim_quote: Pricing is confusing. I genuinely don't understand why my last bill was what it was.

extracted.verbatim_quote: There's like five different ways to filter and they all behave slightly differently.

extracted.claim: The visual distinction between dashboard-level and chart-level filters is too subtle, causing users to lose track of active filters.
extracted.verbatim_quote: sometimes a filter applies to the whole dashboard, sometimes just one chart, and the visual difference is super subtle.

extracted.claim: Forgotten chart-level filters have caused the user to present incorrect data in meetings, creating a real business risk.
extracted.verbatim_quote: I've been on calls where I'm explaining a number and it's actually wrong because I had a chart-level filter on that I forgot about.

extracted.claim: Hidden per-query costs were not visible upfront, making actual pricing higher than expected.
extracted.verbatim_quote: In practice it's been more, because of the per-query stuff that you don't really see until you're a few months in.

extracted.claim: Dashboard load times degrade significantly as the number of charts increases, forcing users to split content across many more dashboards than desired.
extracted.verbatim_quote: dashboard load times are bad once you have a lot of charts. We've had to artificially limit dashboards to six or seven charts each, which means we have like 40 dashboards instead of 15. It's a mess to navigate.

extracted.claim: There is no in-product way to annotate or comment on charts, forcing teams to use external workarounds that quickly become stale.
extracted.verbatim_quote: there's no good way to comment on or annotate a chart for the team. People take screenshots and paste them in Slack with their commentary, and then those screenshots get stale immediately. We need an in-product way to say "here's what changed in week 14 and here's why."

extracted.claim: Salesforce sync fails silently on a regular basis, causing incorrect dashboard data without any alerting to notify users.
extracted.verbatim_quote: Salesforce sync is unreliable. Like at least once a week I get a Slack from someone saying their dashboard is wrong, and 80% of the time it's because the Salesforce sync silently failed. We need either a real fix or at minimum an alert when it breaks.

extracted.claim: Per-query pricing structure lacks upfront transparency and should be made more visible before purchase.
extracted.verbatim_quote: The per-query thing should be more visible upfront.

extracted.verbatim_quote: there's no good way to comment on or annotate a chart for the team. People take screenshots and paste them in Slack with their commentary, and then those screenshots get stale immediately.

extracted.claim: Salesforce sync is unreliable, causing frequent dashboard errors without alerts.
extracted.verbatim_quote: Salesforce sync is unreliable. Like at least once a week I get a Slack from someone saying their dashboard is wrong, and 80% of the time it's because the Salesforce sync silently failed.

extracted.claim: Pricing is confusing due to per-query costs not being visible upfront.
extracted.verbatim_quote: The per-query thing should be more visible upfront.

extracted.claim: The filter UI is deeply nested and hard to find, causing non-technical users to waste significant time on basic tasks.
extracted.verbatim_quote: Took me 15 minutes of clicking around because the filter UI is buried in this dropdown menu inside another dropdown menu.

extracted.claim: Users must leave the app and seek external help to complete basic tasks, indicating a lack of self-serve discoverability.

extracted.verbatim_quote: There's no in-context help, no tooltips. The docs are okay but I don't want to leave the app and read docs to add a filter.

extracted.verbatim_quote: I have an alert set for if returns spike above a threshold, and I get the alert but the dashboard says we're below the threshold. I think it's a timezone thing? But I shouldn't have to debug timezone issues.

extracted.verbatim_quote: I don't trust the numbers anymore. Between the Salesforce sync issues my team has mentioned, and the alert thing, I find myself double-checking everything in the source spreadsheet our finance team maintains. Which kind of defeats the purpose of having a BI tool.

extracted.claim: The Datadog connector only pulls metrics, not events, making it impossible to correlate deploys with error spikes in a single view.

extracted.claim: The product feedback loop is opaque — users who file feature requests receive no meaningful follow-up and don't know if their input has any impact.
extracted.verbatim_quote: Filed a feature request like seven months ago. Got a "we're tracking it" reply. Haven't heard since. That's a separate thing — your product feedback loop is a black box. I don't know if anything I tell you matters.
